title: Pyrrosia nummulariifolia
intro: Pyrrosia nummulariifolia, commonly known as the coin-leaved polypody, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Pyrrosia nummulariifolia include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Pyrrosia nummulariifolia is classified in the family Polypodiaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as polypodiaceous ferns. This family is part of the order Polypodiales, which includes various types of ferns. The genus Pyrrosia is a group of epiphytic ferns, which are plants that grow on other plants. The species Pyrrosia nummulariifolia is native to tropical regions of Asia, and it is often found growing on trees in these areas. The classification of Pyrrosia nummulariifolia provides insight into its evolutionary history and its relationships with other plants.
uses: Pyrrosia nummulariifolia, also known as the coin-leaf pyrrosia, has been traditionally used in various folk remedies in Southeast Asia, particularly in China and Indonesia. Its leaves are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and protect against oxidative stress. In traditional medicine, the plant is used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The leaves are typically harvested and dried for use in teas, infusions, or as a topical application. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Pyrrosia nummulariifolia for medicinal purposes.

The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Triterpenoids, specifically, have been shown to have potential anti-cancer and antimicrobial properties. These active constituents work together to provide the plant's medicinal benefits and support overall health.
